test_description: For each masked nside=256 temperature map, remove the best-fit monopole and dipole on the unmasked sky, reconstruct or retain only multipoles 2 <= ell <= 20, and synthesize the corresponding low-l map. Split unmasked pixels into fixed ecliptic north and south hemispheres. In each hemisphere H, compute the weighted skewness gamma_H = <(T-mu_H)^3>_H / <(T-mu_H)^2>_H^(3/2), using the same mask weights as for the simulations. The single anomaly statistic is S = gamma_South - gamma_North. Compare the Planck SMICA value of S to the distribution of S from the 1000 LCDM simulations.
test_hypothesis: The Planck low-l (2 <= ell <= 20) temperature map has an anomalous ecliptic hemispherical skewness contrast, meaning S = gamma_South - gamma_North is unusually different from the LCDM simulation expectation.
justificaton: The statistic is signed, with positive values indicating excess southern skewness and negative values indicating excess northern skewness, but the test description does not specify a strong prior preference for either direction. The code evaluates both upper and lower tails and also reports two-sided p-values, so the natural anomaly hypothesis is a deviation in either direction.

custom_summary.test_description: Low-l Ecliptic Hemispherical Skewness Contrast: for each masked nside=256 temperature map, remove the best-fit monopole and dipole on the unmasked sky, retain only multipoles 2 <= ell <= 20, synthesize the low-l map, and return S = gamma_South - gamma_North, the fixed ecliptic hemispherical weighted-skewness contrast.
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: upper tail for excess southern ecliptic low-l skewness relative to the north; lower tail for northern excess
custom_summary.monopole_dipole_treatment: best-fit monopole and Cartesian dipole are removed on the unmasked sky before harmonic filtering
custom_summary.ecliptic_coordinate_assumption: Input HEALPix maps are in Galactic coordinates; fixed ecliptic hemispheres use the J2000 ecliptic north pole at Galactic (l,b)=(96.384,29.812) deg.

plot_spec.title: Low-l ecliptic hemispherical skewness contrast
plot_spec.caption: Distribution of the ell=2..20 low-l ecliptic hemispherical skewness contrast. Positive values indicate larger weighted skewness in the southern ecliptic hemisphere.

custom_summary.interpretation: Planck is not in a 5% one-sided tail for this fixed ecliptic low-l skewness contrast.

test_summary.Description: After masked monopole/dipole removal, the map was filtered to 2 <= ell <= 20 and split into fixed ecliptic north/south hemispheres. The statistic was S = gamma_South - gamma_North, using weighted hemispherical skewness.
test_summary.Results: Planck gives S = -0.214, compared with LCDM simulations having mean 0.010 and std 0.303. The Planck value is z = -0.74, at the 21.3rd percentile, with two-tailed p = 0.426 from 1000 simulations.
test_summary.Interpretation: Planck shows a mild negative contrast, i.e. slightly larger northern than southern ecliptic skewness, but this is well within the simulated LCDM distribution and far from a 2 sigma deviation.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: No directly matching published statistic was identified in prior searches. Related CMB anomaly literature discusses low-l alignments, ecliptic features, hemispherical asymmetry, and non-Gaussianity, but not this exact fixed-ecliptic low-l hemispherical skewness-contrast test.
